back: A transaction is a logical unit of work containing one or more SQL operations that are executed as a single atomic unit. Either all operations succeed or none do.

front: What does ACID stand for in transaction properties?
back: ACID stands for Atomicity (all-or-nothing), Consistency (valid state to valid state), Isolation (concurrent transactions appear serial), and Durability (committed changes persist).
Active, Partially Committed, Committed, Failed, and Aborted. A transaction moves through these states during its lifecycle.

front: What is the difference between COMMIT and ROLLBACK?
back: COMMIT makes all changes permanent in the database, saving the transaction. ROLLBACK undoes all changes since the last COMMIT or SAVEPOINT, reverting to previous state.

front: What is a SAVEPOINT in SQL?
back: SAVEPOINT creates a named checkpoint within a transaction allowing partial rollback to that point without aborting the entire transaction.
A dirty read occurs when a transaction reads uncommitted data from another concurrent transaction that might later be rolled back, leading to invalid data.
Non-repeatable read happens when a transaction reads the same row twice but gets different values because another transaction modified and committed that row between the reads.
Phantom read occurs when a transaction re-executes a query and gets a different set of rows because another transaction inserted or deleted rows.

front: What is READ UNCOMMITTED isolation level?
back: The lowest isolation level where transactions can read uncommitted changes from other transactions. Prone to all three read phenomena (dirty, non-repeatable, phantom).

front: What is READ COMMITTED isolation level?
back: Default in Oracle and SQL Server. Transactions can only read committed data, preventing dirty reads but allowing non-repeatable and phantom reads.

front: What is REPEATABLE READ isolation level?
back: Ensures that if a row is read once, it will return the same value in subsequent reads within the transaction. Prevents dirty and non-repeatable reads but allows phantom reads.
Highest isolation level that executes transactions as if they ran sequentially. Prevents all three read phenomena but may cause performance degradation due to locking.
A shared lock allows multiple transactions to read a resource simultaneously but prevents any transaction from modifying it until all shared locks are released.
An exclusive lock grants full ownership to a single transaction to both read and modify a resource. No other transaction can acquire any lock on that resource.

front: What is a deadlock in databases?
back: A deadlock occurs when two or more transactions are waiting indefinitely for each other to release locks, creating a circular dependency. DBMS must detect and resolve it.
